name: People Ops
description: Your people-ops helpdesk. Answers team policy questions (with a classifier that escalates legal-sensitive topics to a human lawyer), drafts first-90-days onboarding plans, pulls employee dossiers, runs approval flows, and keeps the compliance calendar — I-9s, W-4s, visa renewals, review cycles.
author: Houston Founder HR

agentSeeds..houston/activity.json: [{"id":"f616c6ec-6dae-410f-9c03-3b5b2330f5cf","title":"Onboard me — 3 quick questions (~90s)","description":"Click this card, then send any message to kick off onboarding. I'll ask three things: where your employee records live (HRIS), where team members ask HR questions (helpdesk channel), and where your policy docs live. Best modality per topic: connected app via Composio (Integrations tab) > file/URL > paste.","status":"needs_you"}]

useCases.title: Answer a team policy question with escalation routing
useCases.prompt: Does {employee} qualify for {PTO / leave / reimbursement / equipment}?
useCases.fullPrompt: Answer this policy question: {question}. Use the answer-policy-question skill. Read the policy canon AND the escalation rules from ../head-of-people/people-context.md. Classify the question into one of three buckets: (1) direct answer — covered by canon, draft the reply citing the policy section and read voice notes for tone; (2) ambiguous — canon silent or unclear, draft a recommended answer AND flag as 'needs founder review'; (3) escalation required — matches the escalation rules (discrimination, harassment, wage disputes, visa law, protected-class performance actions), do NOT draft a policy answer, draft the escalation note routing to founder / human lawyer. Save to policy-answers/{slug}.md with the classification recorded, log to outputs.json.
useCases.description: Reads the policy canon + escalation rules from the shared people-context doc. Classifies into direct / ambiguous / escalation — never drafts legal-sensitive answers directly.
useCases.outcome: Classified reply at policy-answers/{slug}.md with the bucket recorded (direct / ambiguous / escalation).

useCases.title: Draft the first-90-days plan for a new hire
useCases.prompt: Draft the onboarding plan for {new hire} starting {date} — role: {role}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft the first-90-days onboarding plan for {new hire} starting {date} in the {role} role. Use the onboard-new-hire skill. Read ../head-of-people/people-context.md for the leveling framework, values, and voice notes. Produce: Day 0 prep (accounts to provision, equipment to ship, buddy assignment, calendar blocks), Week 1 (welcome-packet, intro meetings with founder + team + cross-functional, tooling walkthrough), and Day 30 / 60 / 90 milestones with deliverables + check-in prompts pulled from the leveling framework. Draft the welcome Slack message + welcome email in the company voice. Save to onboarding-plans/{new-hire-slug}.md and log in outputs.json.
useCases.description: Reads leveling framework + voice from people-context. Produces Day 0 provisioning, Week 1 intros, and 30/60/90 milestones — plus the welcome Slack and email drafts.
useCases.outcome: First-90-days plan at onboarding-plans/{new-hire-slug}.md with welcome Slack + email drafted.

useCases.title: Pull the full dossier on an employee
useCases.prompt: Tell me everything we know about {employee} — prep me for our 1:1.
useCases.fullPrompt: Aggregate a full dossier on {employee}. Use the employee-dossier skill. Pull role / tenure / comp / manager / location from the connected HRIS (read-only — never modify HRIS records). Cross-reference my own onboarding-plans/{employee-slug}.md, Performance & Retention's checkins/ entries, and Recruiter's interview-loops/{employee-slug}.md if they were a past candidate. Produce a single-page dossier with four sections: profile, history, recent signals (1:1s, retention scores, approvals), upcoming (review date, visa expiry, equity cliff, milestone). Save to dossiers/{employee-slug}.md and log in outputs.json. Never share one employee's confidential data with another without explicit authorization.
useCases.description: Aggregates HRIS profile, onboarding history, recent 1:1 signals, and upcoming milestones into one scannable page — great prep before a 1:1, review, or sensitive conversation.

useCases.title: Review a PTO / comp / promotion request against our rubric
useCases.prompt: Review this {PTO / comp / promotion / expense} request against our rubric.
useCases.fullPrompt: Evaluate this request: {request details}. Use the run-approval-flow skill. Read ../head-of-people/people-context.md for the relevant rubric (PTO policy, comp bands, promotion criteria, expense thresholds). Classify the request as approved / escalate / denied with written reasoning tied to specific rubric clauses. For escalations (comp delta beyond band, PTO beyond policy, promotion outside cycle), produce the escalation note naming exactly who it needs to go to per the escalation rules. Never approve a request that falls outside the rubric without surfacing the exception for founder sign-off. Save to approvals/{request-slug}.md and log in outputs.json.
useCases.description: Evaluates a request against the rubric in people-context. Classifies approved / escalate / denied with reasoning. Escalations get a routed note instead of a decision.
useCases.outcome: Decision memo at approvals/{request-slug}.md with classification + reasoning.

useCases.title: Build and keep the compliance calendar current
useCases.blurb: I-9s · W-4s · visa renewals · review cycles · vesting cliffs.
useCases.prompt: Build the compliance calendar — I-9s, W-4s, visa renewals, review cycles.
useCases.fullPrompt: Build or refresh the compliance calendar. Use the compliance-calendar skill. Scan employee records via the connected HRIS (read-only), read the review-cycle rhythm from ../head-of-people/people-context.md, and check open visa / work-authorization statuses. Produce calendar entries for: I-9 deadlines (3-day rule), W-4 refresh timing, visa expirations with 90 / 60 / 30 day warnings, state registration requirements, review-cycle dates, equity vesting cliffs (notify pre-cliff), PTO policy refresh dates. The living doc lives at compliance-calendar.md at the agent root — update atomically in place. Log each substantive update to outputs.json as type: compliance.
useCases.description: Scans HRIS + people-context review rhythm + visa statuses. Produces a living calendar with deadline warnings. Updated atomically in place at compliance-calendar.md.
useCases.outcome: Living compliance calendar at compliance-calendar.md (updated atomically) + per-update entries in outputs.json.
